expectations.evidence: Without the skill, naive Claude has no structured model-selection dialog (Step 2.5), no {CHANGE_MODELS} map, and no AskUserQuestion prompts for per-change model choice. A user could specify models in their request text and naive Claude might interpret them, but there is no systematic per-change selection flow.
Without the skill's Step 4 which explicitly passes model: {CHANGE_MODELS}[change-name], naive Claude would not know to set different models per subagent. All subagents would use the default model.

expectations.text: Reports which model each change used
expectations.evidence: Without the progress table and final report templates, naive Claude would not include per-change model information in its output. There is no incentive for naive Claude to track and report model assignments systematically.

expectations.text: Falls back to default if not specified
expectations.evidence: Naive Claude would use its own default model for all spawned subagents if no model is specified. This is basic assistant behavior — not skill-specific. However, the default would not be documented or surfaced to the user.

notes: The structured model selection flow (three strategies, per-change {CHANGE_MODELS} map, model in progress table) is entirely skill-specific. Without it, model selection is implicit and undocumented.
